meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Freelancers: The Ultimate Guide
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing to overcome lack of SaaS knowledge. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade subscription models in record time.
content.answer_block: Overcoming a lack of SaaS knowledge requires shifting focus from complex architectures to proven pricing patterns. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid boilerplate generation and Claude Code for iterative logic refinement, you can deploy a professional-grade, Stripe-integrated subscription engine that maximizes client ROI without needing an engineering degree.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your client’s launch, and the dreaded 'How do we charge for this?' conversation is looming. You’re a builder, not a legacy software architect, and the sheer complexity of subscription management, tax compliance, and billing tiers feels like a brick wall standing between you and the finish line. 

But here is the industry secret: you don't need a PhD in SaaS economics to build a high-converting pricing engine. By treating pricing as a modular component rather than a monolithic build, you can strip away the technical debt before it even starts. Let’s turn that uncertainty into your most valuable service offering.

steps.title: Mapping the Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Before writing code, define three tiers: Starter (Growth), Pro (Scale), and Enterprise (Custom). This structure prevents feature creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a JSON schema for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers: Starter, Pro, and Enterprise, including feature arrays, monthly/yearly pricing constants, and Stripe price IDs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a TypeScript interface based on the generated pricing JSON schema to type-check subscription plans.'

steps.title: Scaffolding the Stripe Integration
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate for Stripe Webhooks, which are essential for syncing your app state with payment status.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Next.js API route for Stripe webhook processing that handles 'checkout.session.completed' and 'customer.subscription.updated' events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add stripe-webhook-handler.ts --context 'Ensure secure signature verification using stripe.webhooks.constructEvent.'

steps.title: Implementing Middleware Protection
steps.description: Secure your application routes based on subscription status to ensure users only access the features they have paid for.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a middleware function that checks a user's subscription status from the database and redirects non-paying users to the pricing page.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor middleware.ts --logic 'Apply subscription-based route protection for /dashboard/* paths.'

steps.title: Building the Dynamic Pricing UI
steps.description: Create a responsive, high-converting pricing table component that dynamically switches between monthly and yearly billing cycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Tailwind CSS pricing table component with a toggle switch for yearly/monthly plans and hover effects for the 'Select' button.
steps.claude_code_command: claude generate component PricingTable.tsx --style 'modern, clean UI similar to Linear or Vercel pricing pages.'

steps.title: Testing the Subscription Flow
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write end-to-end tests that simulate a user journey from the pricing page to a successful Stripe checkout.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Playwright test script that navigates to the pricing page, selects the Pro plan, and confirms the Stripe checkout URL redirection.

pro_tips: Always default to yearly billing; the improved cash flow and lower churn rate are massive selling points for your clients.
Use 'Feature Flagging' to hide enterprise features for lower-tier users rather than hard-coding visibility logic.
Implement a 'grace period' logic in your webhook handler to prevent immediate service disruption on failed payment retries.
Leverage Stripe's Customer Portal to offload billing management (cancellations/upgrades) so you don't have to code those UI screens yourself.
Use standard ISO currency codes in your database to ensure your app is ready for international expansion from day one.
content.next_steps: After deploying your pricing model, focus on setting up automated email receipts and dunning management to reduce churn.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build professional SaaS pricing that scales. Stop overthinking the architecture and start shipping value that your clients will pay a premium for. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
